item.title: Experts bust common food myths, including one that half of all Aussies believe
item.description: It is one of many myths the Food Information Safety Council is looking to bust.

item.title: COVID-19 vaccines carry substantially less risk to kids' heart health than infection, new study finds
item.description: Children who catch COVID-19 are at risk of contracting dangerous heart inflammation, but don't face the same danger from getting vaccinated against the virus.

item.title: Experimental daily pill may lower cholesterol in patients who don't see enough benefit with statins alone
item.description: It is similar to other pills available in Australia already.

item.title: 'Never wash raw chicken': More than half of Australians risking higher chance of food poisoning with common ha
item.description: P﻿eople think this measure is protecting them, but experts warn it could do the opposite.

item.title: Brisbane and Gold Coast residents warned after measles cases detected at concert, tourist attractions
item.description: Residents of two major cities are being told to be on high alert.

item.title: Cost of treatment for deadly cancer slashed dramatically after PBS addition
item.description: Those with multiple myeloma, an incurable blood cancer, would previously pay up to $150,000 for treatment.

item.title: Amyl and the Sniffers' free gig shut down over security fears at Fed Square
item.description: The band paid for $35,000 worth of drink for disappointed fans.

item.title: Parents of teens tragically killed by methanol poisoning team up with government for schoolies abroad warning
item.description: The mothers of two Melbourne teenagers who tragically died last year have teamed up with the government in a warning campaign for young travellers.

item.title: New mRNA flu vaccine far more effective than existing jabs, but has some limitations, study finds
item.description: A newly developed mRNA-based flu vaccine is almost 35 per cent more effective against the most common form of influenza, according to a new study.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Australia's most trusted and distrusted brands revealed as local favourites dominate
item.description: S﻿ome brands soared and others struggled.

item.title: Business owner sends premier $24,000 bill after new public holiday announced
item.description: "I'm not arguing against a public holiday, but we were given four months' notice, so we never budgeted for this in our costings."

item.title: Australian markets suffer $90 billion wipeout as experts issue stark warning
item.description: At one stage today, the ASX had sunk by as much as 4.3 per cent, which would have put it on track for the steepest single-day drop since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 over marketing emails
item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.title: Aussie software giant Atlassian cuts 1600 jobs in push to AI
item.description: The CEO, one of Australia's richest men, said employees would get an email "within 20 minutes" to find out if they had lost their jobs.

item.title: Australia's No.1 retailer named as last year's winner plunges
item.description: T﻿he new king of brick-and-mortar retailers has been decided.

item.title: Man in intensive care after brawl at Melbourne pizza shop
item.description: A Melbourne man is in intensive care after a brawl broke out at a pizza shop in front of shocked diners.

item.title: At least 23,000 jobs in tech have been lost to AI this year. Experts say worse is still to come
item.description: T﻿ech company bosses have had similar things to say about AI.

item.title: Atlassian accused of illegally firing employee who suggested boss was 'rich jerk'
item.description: P﻿rosecutors argued that the employee was merely acting in the company's "Open Company, No Bullshit" philosophy.

item.title: Iconic Stonewall Hotel goes under after 28 years as administrators appointed
item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.title: 'I don't accept it': Kyle Sandilands reveals contract terminated by ARN
item.description: T﻿he shock jock said ARN "decided to try and burn the place down" over an on-air spat with Jackie O.

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies aged 43
item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.title: Why we can continue to call our cheese feta, but fontina will be a no-go
item.description: T﻿he names used in dozens of common goods will change under new rules in the recently inked Australia-European Union trade agreement.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands addresses media after exiting court in $100 million showdown with ARN
item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.title: Businesses urge government to decrease $3 billion tax to avoid 'even worse outcomes' in fuel crisis
item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.title: Apple almost went broke – now it's celebrating 50 years
item.description: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

item.title: Australia to phase out discounted junior wages for young workers
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said the landmark ruling was a "great outcome" for workers across key sectors.

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.title: RBA to end card surcharges as cash usage dwindles
item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.title: 'Doesn't pass the pub test': Hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges
item.description: Inside the hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges.

item.title: 'It has been a privilege': Cusack's Furniture to close late 2026
item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.title: New COVID strain circulating in Australia as it spreads across the globe
item.description: Experts say there are no signs the variant BA.3.2, otherwise known as "Cicada", poses a bigger health threat than other variations.

item.title: Shopper caught trying to defraud Aussie small business using AI photos of 'damaged' order
item.description: "The more I looked at it, the more I discovered red flags."

item.title: Sydney tech giant Canva snaps up two local AI companies as $60 billion company teases 'even bigger' announceme
item.description: A﻿ustralian tech giant Canva has snapped up two local artificial intelligence (AI) companies. But bigger news is on the way.

item.title: Australian airlines among 'most exposed' if jet fuel runs dry
item.description: T﻿heir bottom line will be impacted by any prolonged disruption of aviation fuel, analysts warn.

item.description: T﻿he CEO admitted "the roles people play are shifting".

item.title: Australian small business owners sound the alarm over AI 'reverse scams' that could end up costing you
item.description: Alarm bells went off in small business owner Tanielle's mind as soon as she saw it.

item.title: Three in four Aussie workers skip this simple check – and it could cost them thousands
item.description: Payslip complacency has reached remarkable highs.

item.title: Virgin to cut flights, raise costs after $40 million fuel hit
item.description: Virgin Australia has joined Qantas in cutting flights and hiking ticket prices as skyrocketing fuel prices hit local airlines.

item.title: Big hint Apple's first foldable smartphone is coming this year
item.description: F﻿ollowing years of rumours, Apple may be about to deliver a missing link in its iPhone range.

item.title: Inside 'kitty flipping', the recreational drug trend booming in Australian cities
item.description: A﻿ nation-wide study of our wastewater and online search data has revealed the worrying trend is booming.

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down after 15 years
item.description: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.

item.title: What does the National Disability Insurance Scheme overhaul mean for you?
item.description: The government is set to slash the cost of running the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and aged care under sweeping new changes.

item.title: How a crummy cake kicked off a food-delivery crisis in China
item.description: T﻿he inquiry has been marked by scuffles between investigators and delivery service employees, a feigned medical emergency and hastily scribbled notes to "stay silent".

item.title: The fun photos of Harry and Meghan that could land Aussie small businesses in hot water
item.description: They seem harmless upon first glance, but look a little closer and you'll see the truth.

item.title: Warner Bros shareholders approve Paramount's $113.5 billion takeover of the Hollywood giant
item.description: Many critics have sounded the alarm on joining two of Hollywood's biggest remaining five studios.

item.title: Tech giant Microsoft unveiles $25 billion plan to reshape the Australian workplace
item.description: A﻿nd the man in charge says Australia is in a prime position for the major growth technology.

item.title: While other retailers close up shop, business is booming for these once-taboo stores
item.description: People used to shame it, but clearly something is working for Aussie shoppers.

item.title: Measles alert issues after infectious traveller from Japan visits Sydney
item.description: Anyone who has been to these locations recently should monitor for symptoms, authorities say.

item.title: Man accused of killing Labor strategist to fight charge
item.description: A young man accused of the one-punch killing of political strategist Tim Picton near a nightclub in the early hours has pleaded not guilty to manslaughter.

item.title: Woolworths sales surge but supermarket cuts profit forecast amid petrol price spike
item.description: Woolworths has seen a jump in grocery sales but doesn't expect it to last, slashing its profit forecasts amid high petrol prices.

item.title: Former federal Labor leader Mark Latham ordered to pay $100k for offensive tweet to MP
item.description: T﻿he former federal Labor leader will be forced to pay Alex Greenwich $100,000 after a tweet directed at him was found to be unlawful sexual harassment.

item.title: 'Double whammy' leaves 4.4 million Aussies paying up to $815 more a year
item.description: A proposed private health insurance rebate cut and increased premiums are hitting the country's most vulnerable.

item.title: First report into antisemitism royal commission recommends national gun buyback scheme, more counter-terrorism
item.description: T﻿he first report from a royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion in Australia has been published today, less than five months after 15 innocent people were gunned down the Bondi Beach terror attack.

item.title: Syria says Australia won't repatriate families from camp for those with alleged ties to ISIS
item.description: T﻿he Australian government refused to repatriate women and children who had secured passports and a flight out of Syria.

item.title: Ex-Liberal MP continues fight against child sex charges
item.description: Rory Amon has consistently argued he thought a 13-year-old boy was of legal age before meeting him at the youth's apartment block in 2017.

item.title: 'National disgrace': Jacinta Price reacts to death of five-year-old niece
item.description: T﻿he suspected murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby sparks debate among the country's top leaders.

item.title: 'A soft target for crooks': Shocking extent of NDIS fraud revealed
item.description: "If you think it is acceptable to prey on people with disabilities, you do not belong in the NDIS. You belong in prison, and we are coming for you."

item.title: Residents of seaside Victorian town braced for Macca's battle
item.description: R﻿esidents of Philip Island in Victoria are bracing for a dual battle to stop the stores from opening.

item.description: H﻿e has also expanded on a threat to remove troops from a European nation that is a key ally.

item.title: Victorian Liberal Party holds onto Nepean after gliding to byelection victory
item.description: The byelection had been regarded as Liberal Leader Jess Wilson's first test ahead of the November state election.

item.title: Trump keeps everyone up in the air with hints of what's coming in new batch of UFO files
item.description: H﻿e's generating a mix of buzz and scepticism as he hints at new revelations around questions of alien life.

item.description: B﻿oth countries have agreed to strengthen economic, energy and defence ties.

item.title: Click Frenzy saved from collapse in eleventh-hour bid by e-commerce veterans
item.description: B﻿ut it's not all rosy for the seasoned sales platform.

item.title: AS IT HAPPENED: Reserve Bank gives the news no mortgage-holder wants
item.description: The Reserve Bank has done what homeowners were dreading by hiking interest rates again to 4.35 per cent.

item.title: 'Easier. Safer. More affordable': All the winners and losers from this year's Victorian budget
item.description: Victoria has unveiled its pre-election budget, delivering its first operating surplus in seven years under the promise of "a household with a bit more room at the end of the week".

item.title: Banks hit weary customers with fresh rate rise following RBA call
item.description: Australia's big four banks have wasted little time in passing on today's interest rate hike to their customers.

item.title: Bleak rate hike assessment: 'Australians are poorer' and 'everything is moving in the wrong direction'
item.description: The Reserve Bank's third successive rate hike hit mortgage holders like a kick in the guts and there is little light to be found at the end of the tunnel when digging deeper.

item.title: Australian author Craig Silvey's books permanently pulled from WA public schools
item.description: O﻿ne state has moved quickly to make a temporary ban permanent after the disgraced author pleaded guilty to child exploitation charges.

item.title: Kids deserve 'second chance' senator says as ISIS-linked cohort returns
item.description: The group have spent years in Syrian refugee camps, but some will be arrested on their return.

item.description: T﻿he pair were in a verbal altercation, before is escalated.

item.description: The organisation behind of Australia's largest and longest-standing early stage start-up communities has co﻿llapsed into voluntary administration.

item.title: Investors slap down ARN executive pay packets amid ugly Kyle and Jackie O legal fight
item.description: It's the worst remuneration strike in recent Australian corporate history in a stunning blow to the embattled network.

item.description: The proposed move is hoped to boost supply and reduce prices.

item.title: ISIS bride whisked away by police moments after landing
item.description: A group of so-called "ISIS brides" and their children will land in Australia today after more than a decade...

item.title: All eyes on Farrer: How regional electorate could upend conservative politics
item.description: How the result in Farrer could send shockwaves across nation and dismantle the Coalition.

item.title: Former foreign minister Julie Bishop quits as ANU chancellor after months of turmoil
item.description: Bishop reportedly tendered her resignation﻿ to the university and Albanese government last night seven months before her tenure was due to expire in December.

item.title: More cases of hantavirus possible but risk is 'low', leading health body says
item.description: More hentavirus cases could emerge in the coming weeks but the risk to public health is "low", according to the World Health Organisation.

item.title: 'Honest mistakes': Anika Wells ordered to repay over $10,000 in travel expenses after parliamentary audit
item.description: Anika Wells has repaid $10,116 to the public purse for four taxpayer-funded trips, which included $8093 in expenses and a $2023 penalty.

item.title: 'We're not leaving': How one of Sydney's most popular go-karting tracks overturned a council plan to become a
item.description: The carpark construction, which would have created an additional 296 parking spots, would have come at the expense of the Hyper Karting track, axing more than 150 jobs in the process.

item.title: Sydney 'ISIS bride' facing terror charges has bail bid rejected
item.description: Janai Safar, 32, appeared in Sydney Downing Centre via video link today, where she was refused bail over charges relating to allegedly entering and remaining in a declared conflict zone and joining ISIS.

item.title: 'One big glow': Trump's horrifying threat of apocalypse
item.description: The president said one thing would make it clear that the ceasefire was over.

item.title: 'Historic change in British politics': Hard-right Reform surges as Labour takes big losses in local elections
item.description: Partial results from local elections in England show big losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's governing Labour Party and gains for the hard-right party Reform UK.

item.title: 'We serve the local community': ABC journalists booted from One Nation event on eve of crucial byelection
item.description: Pauline Hanson has bristled at her chief of staff's controversial decision on the eve of a crucial byelection.

item.title: Multiple hikers killed, others rescued after volcano erupts in Indonesia
item.description: A rescue operation is underway to locate 20 missing hikers after a deadly volcanic eruption in Indonesia, local officials said Friday.

item.title: Tiny gap in the ocean could become the world's most expensive tollbooth
item.description: Iran's attempts to exact a hefty toll on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz has given other countries an idea.

item.title: One Nation's performance in Farrer could change Australian politics as we know it
item.description: All eyes are on the regional NSW seat of Farrer today as Pauline Hanson's One Nation faces its first federal litmus test.

item.title: Today in History - May 9: Two identical bodies found in search for world-famous magician
item.description: Perhaps the world's most famous magician at the time was killed in a theatre fire on May 9, 1911.

item.title: How millions are using prediction markets and stocks to try to profit from war
item.description: Millions of people are attempting to profit from the conflict through well-timed bets and carefully picked stocks. ﻿

item.title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for a three-day ceasefire and a prisoner swap
item.description: President Donald Trump said that the leaders of Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for a three-day ceasefire.

item.title: Trump celebrates decision that could secure his party's re-election months before voting takes place
item.description: Donald Trump has celebrated after a court decision that will cost Democrats dearly in the upcoming midterm elections.

item.title: Small businesses and tradies to permanently keep $20,000 tax break in federal budget
item.description: The $20,000 threshold will apply per asset and will expand across tools, technology or other assets, both new and second-hand, and can be claimed in full at the end of every financial year.﻿

item.title: Pentagon won't deny attack after massive oil slick spotted off Kharg Island
item.description: A massive oil slick spotted off Kharg Island has sparked speculation the US has targeted civilian infrastructure on the vital Iranian export hub.

item.title: Trump's company loses half a billion dollars in three months
item.description: Donald Trump's media company lost more than four hundred times more money than it made from January to March this year.

item.title: Negative gearing set to undergo first change in nearly 40 years. Here's what you need to know
item.description: You have probably heard about negative gearing quite a bit recently, and that's because the popular tax policy will likely be reformed for the first time in almost 40 years in next week's federal budget.

item.title: Australia prepares to repatriate citizens as hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for the Canary Islands
item.description: The Dutch-flagged vessel is set to dock on the island of Tenerife around midday tomorrow, local time (9pm AEDT).

item.title: 'A bomb going off in Canberra': After stunning byelection result, what now for One Nation and the Coalition?
item.description: Farrer may be an electorate of only about 180,000 people, but One Nation's byelection victory in the conservative heartland is sending shockwaves across the country.

item.title: Today in History - May 10: Bank robber's identity a mystery after sniper blows him to smithereens
item.description: A bank robber was blown to pieces after a police sniper's bullet detonated the dynamite he was carrying in a small Canadian town on May 10, 1973.

item.title: Iran warns the US against attacks on its oil tankers and other ships but ceasefire appears to hold
item.description: H﻿owever, the ceasefire still appears to be holding.

item.title: Australian-born MP threatens to topple British prime minister
item.description: C﻿atherine West has thrown down the gauntlet to Keir Starmer following crushing local election defeats for the ruling Labour Party.

item.title: 'We just hit somebody': Person dies after being hit by Frontier Airlines plane taking off at Denver airport
item.description: T﻿he impact caused an engine fire, forcing take off to be aborted and passengers to evacuate.

item.title: Kristin Smart's remains not found in search of California home, authorities say
item.description: Authorities did not find the remains of Kristin Smart, a 19-year-old college student who went missing in 1996, officials said on Saturday after finishing a search of a home tied to the man convicted of killing her.

item.title: Top Liberal hints he's open to One Nation joining Coalition after stunning byelection triumph
item.description: Voters in Farrer sent a damning message to the Coalition, and it highlights grim questions for its political future.

item.title: Iran war could make Trump's trip to China a bit chillier than his first-term visit
item.description: Weeks before his trip to China, US President Donald Trump was already predicting on social media that his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping, would "give me a big, fat hug when I get there".

item.description: "I just feel that there was no future for our generations to come."

item.description: The group will be forced to quarantine for at least three weeks, with a longer period likely.

item.description: Katy Gallagher has been criticised for saying children are better off the earlier they enter early education or childcare.

item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has insisted he has no intention of resigning as calls grew louder within his Labour Party for him to step down and some junior members of his government quit in protest.

item.description: It brought high fashion to Australia, had the nation's first escalator and even a ballroom, where film star Elizabeth Taylor once partied.

item.description: Donald Trump has brushed off the financial concerns of American voters when asked about his continuing war with Iran.

item.title: Football academy praises 'much-loved' student who became trapped under train
item.description: An elite Melbourne football academy has thrown its support behind a Year 7 Melbourne schoolboy who was pulled onto train tracks in a horror accident after his bag became snagged.

item.title: Retired NSW police commissioner Karen Webb to lead review after Kumanjayi Little Baby's death
item.description: Retired NSW Police Commissioner Karen Webb will jointly lead a review into the Northern Territory's child protection system sparking by the alleged murder of five-year-old Alice Springs girl, Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.description: A spokesperson for Zoos Victoria's Marine Response Unit said there is a "strong likelihood" that the deceased seal is Sammy.

item.description: The ACCC said ﻿traces of anthophyllite asbestos and tremolite asbestos were detected in the toy after testing.﻿

item.description: As America was getting ready for work, Donald Trump was busy on Truth Social, posting a flurry of AI-generated images.

item.description: Police say the discovery of the body provides solace for a town shattered by the shooting.

item.title: More charges for Perth man accused of mass terror attack plan
item.description: A man accused of plotting a mass casualty terror attack targeting public buildings and places of worship has been hit with a slew of new charges, including possession of child abuse material.

item.description: Two key Iranian figures have insisted the country will be holding firm in their demands if - or when - peac...

item.title: French hantavirus patient is critically ill and on an artificial lung, hospital reveals
item.description: A French hantavirus patient is critically ill and is using an artificial lung to breathe, it's been revealed, as the number of ﻿people suspected to have contracted the deadly virus grows to 11.

item.title: Man critically injured after plot to blow up ATM backfires
item.description: An alleged would-be thief has suffered life-changing injuries after a homemade bomb was used to blow up an ATM in Melbourne's CBD overnight.

item.title: Why it could take years for first buyers to benefit from housing tax reforms
item.description: While the changes were touted as the most significant of the 21st century, it may take years for ﻿lower prices and easier access to the market to reach aspiring first-home buyers. ﻿

item.description: F﻿ine print in the budget has delivered an unwelcome surprise for every Aussie looking to holiday overseas.

item.title: Iran war forces Japanese snack giant Calbee to remove colour from chip packets
item.description: Global conflict has reached a new frontier.

item.title: Queensland toddler 'awake and alert' after days in induced coma
item.description: The mother of a Gold Coast toddler who was put in an induced coma after inhaling cake decorating dust has shared a heartwarming update on his condition.

item.title: Motorbike 'nutcases' across Sydney take to social media as they perform dangerous stunts
item.description: ﻿"Pay with cash, never take your helmet off... and go before they notice you've got no plates."

item.title: All the changes and announcements you might have missed in the 2026 Federal Budget
item.description: The federal government delivered what it has described as its most significant of the 21st century, and aside from sweeping, generational tax reform, there are some key changes hidden in the thousands of pages.

item.title: Thousands of patients of Sydney dentist urged to get tested for bloodborne viruses
item.description: An audit at the practice "identified deficiencies in the sterilisation of equipment and in cleaning practices".

item.title: Australians evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship likely to overstay welcome in the Netherlands
item.description: Australians being repatriated from the virus-struck cruise ship will likely overstay their welcome in the Netherlands.

item.title: Mum set to be charged after using listening device to investigate son's injuries
item.description: T﻿he Victorian mum insists she was only trying to protect her little boy.

item.title: Bikie cops life behind bars for sniper murder of rival
item.description: A bikie who paid an assassin to gun down a rival gang member following a disagreement has been sentenced to life behind bars, but his lawyer says he will appeal his conviction.

item.title: Cop could have been ordered not to Taser great-grandmother, inquest told
item.description: His partner said "oh shit" when he deployed the stun gun and a paramedic felt "absolute shock and disbelief", an inquest has heard.

item.title: Rinehart legal saga reignites, Hancock lodges appeal
item.description: Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart, will fight elements of a complex court ruling that found two rival mining dynasties were entitled to a share of the spoils from some of the billionaire's prized iron ore assets.

item.title: Donald Trump arrives in Beijing for talks with China's Xi Jinping on Iran war, trade and US arms sales to Taiw
item.description: While Trump likes to project a sense of strength, the visit occurs at a delicate moment for his presidency.

item.title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest senator
item.description: Senator Ronald dela Rosa has stayed under the protection of allied senators as Philippine authorities tried to arrest him for a charge of crime against humanity.

item.title: Prosecutors to retry Alex Murdaugh over deaths of wife and son after high court overturned convictions
item.description: Prosecutors say they plan to retry disgraced lawyer Alex Murdaugh after a court found the clerk "egregiously attacked Murdaugh's credibility".

item.title: France confines more than 1700 on British cruise ship in Bordeaux after gastroenteritis outbreak
item.description: More than 1700 passengers and crew on a British cruise ship have been ordered to remain on board after an outbreak of gastrointestinal illness, French authorities say.

item.title: King Charles III lays out UK government agenda as PM Keir Starmer's job hangs in the balance
item.description: On a day when the British government's legislative plans were presented by no less than King Charles III himself, Prime Minister Keir Starmer was fighting to remain in power.

item.title: 'Enormous injustice': One lucky generation of landlords will dodge the budget's major tax change
item.description: A grandfathering policy means investors who owned their property before 7.30pm on May 12 can indefinitely benefit from the friendly tax break.

item.title: Today in History - May 14: House painter never told he was given enormous dose of plutonium
item.description: A house painter with a terminal cancer diagnosis was given the highest dose of radiation ever received by a human on May 14, 1945.

item.title: Heartbroken families say they are being blocked from visiting or video-calling incarcerated loved ones at NSW
item.description: Prisoner advocate and former inmate Renee McNab told Nine.com.au this is an issue impacting jails across the state.

item.title: Two charged with weapons, drugs offences after police raid rural properties
item.description: H﻿eavily armed police yesterday swarmed three rural properties in Victoria.

item.title: 'Most powerful': Putin boasts about nuclear-capable missile
item.description: T﻿he Russian president claims the weapon - known as "Satan II" - can hit targets 35,000km away.

item.title: Taylor to put migration and tax at the centre of his budget reply
item.description: Migrants will get no welfare support until they become citizens and Australia's net immigration intake will be tied to the number of new homes built, under the Coalition's plans.

item.title: 'Terrifying': Two injured after car ploughs into patrons at Melbourne cafe
item.description: "We just heard loud screaming and a loud smashing noise."

item.title: Nearly 900 workers evacuated, five hospitalised after chemical spill at Queensland abattoir
item.description: Emergency services were called to meat processing plant JBS Dinmore on Lockway in Riverview, Ipswich about 12.35am today following reports of a chemical leak, believed to be an ammonia spill.

item.title: The motley crew of billionaires tagging along on Trump's China visit
item.description: With a combined value of well more than a trillion dollars, they all have something to gain from the trip.

item.title: Sons claim they are scared of mother who wrote book on grief after murdering their father
item.description: K﻿ouri Richins wrote a book about grief after the death of her husband Eric, but was convicted of his murder.

item.title: Man charged after woman's body found on Perth street
item.description: Police said he approached paramedics with a body in his arms and took off after asking for help.

item.title: Former Jacqui Lambie senator Tammy Tyrrell defects to Labor
item.description: Tyrrell bluntly told reporters that she is proud to join the major party and would not "apologise to anybody" making the switch.

item.title: Coles 'illusory' discounts misled shoppers, court rules
item.description: T﻿he decision could see the $28 billion supermarket giant hit with nine-figure penalties.

item.title: Australian passengers rescued from Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship to land in Perth tomorrow
item.description: Five Australians – three from NSW and two from Queensland – and one New Zealand national will arrive in the Netherlands at 4pm AEDT today.

item.title: Tradies say federal budget will hurt an already strained construction industry
item.description: The country's peak body says announced tax reforms is "absolutely not what we need right now".

item.title: Police shun dementia training after woman's Taser death
item.description: Then-senior constable Kristian James Samuel White fired his weapon at 95-year-old Clare Nowland after being called to Yallambee Lodge nursing home at Cooma in southern NSW on May 17, 2023.

item.title: Ex-premier's partner banned from contact in rape case
item.description: Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, has been banned from contacting 10 people.

item.title: More than 100 charges against 14-year-old Melbourne girl dropped
item.description: Police alleged in court that the girl had googled "where do Jews live" prior to some of the offences.

item.title: Community mourns 'bright and beautiful' nine-year-old girl who died suddenly from brain bleed
item.description: The Matildas praised the young footballer's spirit. Her football club will wear black armbands in her honour this weekend.

item.title: Young woman charged over 'extremely serious' alleged plot to kill high-profile Alameddine crime figure
item.description: T﻿he 23-year-old's arrest stems from the alleged discovery of weapons and false number plates found in a stolen car.

item.title: Teen who threatened Melbourne synagogue walks free after IS propaganda case
item.description: An 18-year-old who spread Islamic State propaganda online and threatened a synagogue has walked free after spending a year behind bars.

item.title: Man arrested after woman fatally stabbed in Canberra's inner north
item.description: A﻿ 51-year-old man was arrested and remains in hospital under police guard.

item.title: Entertainment news headlines - 9News
item.description: Breaking entertainment news including celebrity gossip, film and movie news, music news, award ceremonies, scandals, photos and videos

item.title: Latest technology news headlines, updates & new tech innovation | 9News
item.description: Latest technology news stories including consumer electronics, science and space technology, gadgets, internet tech, medical devices, websites and social tech, cryptocurrencies and other technology news headlines

item.title: Latest health news top stories & analysis
item.description: Latest health news, research and results, scientific discoveries, diets, obesity, cancer, medical procedures and medicine, hospitals, and government health guidelines

item.title: Business news headlines - 9News
item.description: Latest business news, company tax, government regulations, CEOs, entrepreneurs, customers, and big business strategy

item.title: Politics News - 9News - Latest political news today Australia and World
item.description: Latest political news from Australia and around the world, covering elections, by-elections, our regular leadership spills, poll results, Question Time debates and daily Australian party politics

item.title: World news headlines and international current affairs - 9News
item.description: Latest world news headlines: International breaking news and current affairs from US, Europe, Middle East, Asia, Africa and more. If it happens around the world, you will know about it on 9News

item.title: News Australia - Latest news headlines in Australia
item.description: Today's news in major cities, regional and local areas which can include accident reports, police & emergency responses, criminal and court proceedings or live breaking news

item.title: Lavish welcome for Trump as historic US-China meeting begins
item.description: A﻿ senior member of the Trump administration has disclosed his fears of Iran's capabilities, despite the da...

item.title: Top barrister spared jail time for 'blatant dishonesty'
item.description: The 68-year-old's remorse and longstanding good character meant he could be sufficiently punished without being sent to jail, the judge said.

item.title: Two people in life-threatening condition after bus crash in Queensland
item.description: T﻿wo people are fighting for life after a bus crash on a major highway in Queensland.

item.title: 'Mass migration': Taylor reply to restrict immigrants
item.description: The Opposition Leader is set to unveil his ﻿own plans, the centrepiece of which will be a migration-led pol...
